scope.target: Consistency with already-canonical Handler, time, naming, and spec contracts
constraints: Derive corrections from current canonical standards
Preserve intentionally negative examples as clearly negative
Stop if a new universal decision is required

steps: Align best-practice and anti-pattern Handler examples
Replace inappropriate bare wall-clock examples with the existing abstraction or explicit negative framing
Correct method-naming and retained spec-compliance contract facts
Run focused structural and documentation contract tests
execution.last_completed_step: Corrected Handler, time, naming, connection, and spec-compliance content from existing canonical truth.
execution.next_action: V042-004 resolves navigation and lifecycle hygiene.

results.summary: Positive learning content now separates Use Cases from optional thin Handlers, time examples use the retained DateProvider convention, naming and connection facts are accurate, and spec-compliance guidance is GWT-neutral, sequential, and target-relative.
results.residual_risk: The DateProvider shown here remains an existing example abstraction rather than a newly selected universal time contract; independent verification must confirm no positive contradiction remains.
